nodes.name: End User
nodes.description: Human interacting with the multi-agent application

nodes.name: Web Application
nodes.description: Browser-based front-end serving the agentic experience

nodes.name: Agent Gateway
nodes.description: Entry point for all agentic requests; auth, rate limiting, request routing

nodes.name: Input Guardrail
nodes.description: Validates and filters incoming user prompts before they reach the orchestrator

nodes.name: Agent Orchestrator
nodes.description: Coordinates the trader and risk agents, enforces task hand-off semantics

nodes.name: Trader Agent
nodes.description: Autonomous agent that proposes trades using market data and tools

nodes.name: Risk Agent
nodes.description: Autonomous agent that evaluates proposed trades against risk policy

nodes.name: Vector Store
nodes.description: Embedding-indexed retrieval store for semantic search over historical trades

nodes.name: Risk Policy Knowledge Base
nodes.description: Structured policy documents, regulatory rules, and trading limits

nodes.name: Inference LLM
nodes.description: Foundation model serving completions for the agent layer

nodes.name: Embedding Model
nodes.description: Generates vectors for semantic indexing and retrieval

nodes.name: MCP Tool Server
nodes.description: Model Context Protocol server exposing trade execution and market data tools

nodes.name: Execution Tool
nodes.description: External callable that places orders against an execution venue

nodes.name: Evaluation Monitor
nodes.description: Continuously scores agent outputs against quality and safety metrics

nodes.name: Observability Plane
nodes.description: Telemetry, tracing, and metrics collection for the entire agentic stack

relationships.description: Browser issues authenticated requests to the agent gateway
Inbound prompts pass through input validation
Validated prompts are dispatched to the orchestrator
Orchestrator delegates trade proposal tasks to the trader agent
Orchestrator delegates risk evaluation tasks to the risk agent
Trader agent calls the LLM for reasoning and tool selection
Trader agent retrieves comparable historical trades via semantic search
Trader agent invokes execution-side tools via MCP
Risk agent uses the LLM to reason over policy text
Risk agent looks up policy rules in the structured knowledge base
Vector store generates embeddings for inbound documents on the fly
MCP server exposes the execution tool to authorised agents
Orchestrator emits run summaries to the evaluation monitor
Orchestrator emits traces and metrics for the run
LLM emits inference latency and token-cost metrics
